evals.prompt: Write a function in fp-go v2 that takes a list of User structs (each with Name string and Age int fields), filters to only users over 18, extracts their names, and joins them with a comma. Use pipe, point-free style, and the reader monad with context.Context as the environment. Also generate a unit test.
evals.expected_output: A Go function returning a Reader[context.Context, string] that uses F.Pipe with reader.Do/Bind/ApS or reader.Map, creates lenses or helper functions for Name/Age field access instead of inline lambdas, wraps the result in a function (not a var), and includes a _test.go with assert.Equal. All imports from github.com/IBM/fp-go/v2.

A function processOrder(orderID string) returning ReadierIOResult[context.Context, Invoice] or ReaderIOEither using do-notation with Bind for db calls (sequential, context-dependent) and Let for calculateTotal (pure). Uses helper functions instead of inline lambdas for field access. Returns a function, not a var. Includes _test.go. All imports from github.com/IBM/fp-go/v2.

evals.prompt: I have a Config struct with Host string, Port int, and TLS bool fields. Write fp-go v2 lenses for each field, then write a function that reads the Config from the reader environment and returns a formatted connection string like 'https://host:port'. Use point-free style, no inline functions in the pipe. Generate a test.
evals.expected_output: Three lenses using L.MakeLens from github.com/IBM/fp-go/v2/optics/lens. A function connectionString() returning Reader[Config, string] that uses F.Pipe with R.Ask and R.Map, referencing lens.Get functions by name (not inline). A _test.go that passes a concrete Config struct and checks the returned string. All imports from github.com/IBM/fp-go/v2.
